verdict.answer_first: A opens with “Use a managed provider, not your own”; B opens with “use a managed auth provider, don't roll your own” — both answer first, B is slightly clearer for the exact question.
verdict.decisiveness: A ends with two choices, “Clerk or Supabase Auth”; B gives multiple paths, “Clerk or Supabase... WorkOS or Auth0... Supabase,” so neither gives one clean call, but A is less scattered.
verdict.fabrication: A invents an unsupported concrete timeline: “production in a day”; B has unsupported but non-numeric claims like “gets expensive fast.”
verdict.actionability: A gives a direct rule: “speed-to-ship (Clerk) or low cost and data ownership (Supabase)”; B adds useful lock-in check, but asks the reader to choose across more branches.
